audit_scope: Delta · tone_of_voice + design_compliance only · re-scoring after the cross-page jargon-density comparison surfaced this page as the heaviest-jargon agent page (only one with JSON, SDK, Temporal, FFmpeg, Redis mentions).

tone_of_voice.reason: Placement rule violations · jargon density in hero stat tile, §01 job, §02 SVG outputs + step 06 detail, and §04 pattern descriptions exceeds tone-of-voice §7 'translate at exec read level' threshold.

design_compliance.reason: No new violations · pre-existing template hex/font issues unchanged.

rule_anchor: tone-of-voice §7 · 'Engineering jargon — translate at exec read level' · default: prefer plain English when it carries same information · KEEP brand names paired with reader-recognisable verb · STRICTEST in hero / status / stat tiles · most permissive in §03+ deep-dive (page authored without that placement scale in mind).

findings.issue: Context line of stat tile 3 reads 'Temporal-orchestrated · multi-scene fan-out · auto-grade gate'. 'Temporal-orchestrated' names a backend product an Apex reader doesn't know — this is the strictest zone per §7 ('Hero, status strip, and stat tiles are where the rule bites hardest').
findings.fix: Replace 'Temporal-orchestrated' with 'Durable orchestration'. Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 31 (stats[2].context).
findings.after: Durable orchestration · multi-scene fan-out · auto-grade gate

findings.issue: Stat tile 4 label is 'Provider fabric' (engineering abstraction · 'fabric' is dev jargon), value '9 services' (generic dev term), context lists 9 product names with no grouping. Worst stat tile on the page for an Apex reader. Brand names ARE valid (per §7 keep them) but the framing is opaque.
findings.fix: Re-label and group. Label → 'AI providers'; value → '9'; context → 'Text · Claude · OpenAI · Gemini  ·  Image · Vertex Imagen · fal.ai  ·  Video · Veo · Kling · Runway  ·  CDN · Pixelbin'. Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 32 (stats[3]).
findings.verdict: TRANSLATE (label + value + context · keep brand names)
findings.before: Provider fabric · 9 services · Claude · OpenAI · Gemini · Vertex Imagen · fal.ai · Veo · Kling · Runway · Pixelbin
findings.after: AI providers · 9 · Text (Claude · OpenAI · Gemini)  ·  Image (Vertex Imagen · fal.ai)  ·  Video (Veo · Kling · Runway)  ·  CDN (Pixelbin)

findings.issue: Final sentence reads 'Each stage is a Temporal workflow with retries, signals and durable state.' §01 job-to-be-done is hero-adjacent (high-read zone). 'Temporal workflow' + 'signals' + 'durable state' is three engineering terms in one sentence. The user-value is durability + resumability.
findings.fix: Replace with: 'Each stage is a durable workflow with retries and resumable state.' Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 61.
findings.before: Each stage is a Temporal workflow with retries, signals and durable state.
findings.after: Each stage is a durable workflow with retries and resumable state.

findings.issue: Phrase 'the Shoppable SDK feeds the output into storefronts'. 'SDK' is generic developer noun · the user-value is the embedded widget that retailers add to their storefront.
findings.fix: Replace 'Shoppable SDK' with 'Shoppable widget'. Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 60.
findings.before: the Shoppable SDK feeds the output into storefronts with product hotspots and analytics
findings.after: the Shoppable widget feeds the output into storefronts with product hotspots and analytics

findings.issue: Three of four output cards in the workflow SVG carry developer nouns visible in the rendered diagram: 'Storyboard JSON', 'Video clips · GCS', 'Embeddable SDK widget'. The right band is the most-scanned part of the diagram (eye lands on outputs first) so jargon here costs the most.
findings.fix: (a) 'Storyboard JSON' → 'Storyboard data' (line 322 in YAML). (b) 'Video clips · GCS' → 'Video clips · cloud storage' (line 326). (c) 'Embeddable SDK widget' → 'Embeddable widget' (line 338).

findings.issue: Step 06 detail string is the densest engineering passage on the page — 4 jargon terms in 2 sentences: 'Output ships to GCS, then served through the Shoppable SDK feed (Redis-cached, 60 s) with product hotspots and analytics event tracking. SDK auth via per-brand API keys exchanged for 15-minute JWTs.' Apex reader hits a wall.
findings.fix: Translate all 4 jargon terms. Replace with: 'Output ships to cloud storage, then served through the Shoppable feed (cached for 60-second freshness) with product hotspots and analytics tracking. Per-brand API keys exchanged for short-lived (15-minute) access tokens.' Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 220 (workflow_steps[5].detail).
findings.before: Output ships to GCS, then served through the Shoppable SDK feed (Redis-cached, 60 s) with product hotspots and analytics event tracking. SDK auth via per-brand API keys exchanged for 15-minute JWTs.
findings.after: Output ships to cloud storage, then served through the Shoppable feed (cached for 60-second freshness) with product hotspots and analytics tracking. Per-brand API keys exchanged for short-lived (15-minute) access tokens.

findings.section: §04 Patterns · Wide Research + Context Engineering + Observability chips
findings.issue: Three pattern chips lean on internal code identifiers that aren't in the source code's user-facing surface: 'LongFormVideoWorkflow', 'semaphore-bounded', 'ReferenceProcessor', 'LineageCollector', 'RCAAnalyzer'. These read as Fynd-internal class names exposed to Apex.
findings.fix: Translate the 3 chip bodies. (a) Wide Research: 'LongFormVideoWorkflow fans out parallel frame and video generation per scene (semaphore-bounded, max 3 concurrent), then converges in FFmpeg assembly' → 'Long-form videos fan out parallel frame and video generation per scene (max 3 concurrent), then converge in video assembly'. (b) Context Engineering: 'ReferenceProcessor categorises reference images …' → 'The reference processor categorises reference images (product · character · style) and prepares multimodal inputs for Gemini'. (c) Observability: 'Langfuse trace wraps every LLM, image and video call · LineageCollector traces grading reports back through the pipeline · RCAAnalyzer runs LLM-powered root-cause analysis on failures' → 'Langfuse traces every LLM, image and video call · grading reports trace back through the full pipeline · root-cause analysis runs on failures'.
findings.verdict: TRANSLATE / SOFTEN (drop the code-identifier framing; concepts stay)

findings.issue: Step 02 detail uses code-style backticks and array syntax: 'ScriptWriterAgent runs in `multi_scene` mode — generates `narrative_arc`, `scenes[]`, `visual_treatment`, `captions`'. Reads as a code snippet, not Apex prose.
findings.fix: Drop backticks, replace underscores with spaces, drop array brackets. Replace with: 'Script Writer agent runs in multi-scene mode — generates narrative arc, scenes, visual treatment and captions. Routes through Claude (primary) with OpenAI / Gemini fallback.' Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 217.

findings.issue: Pattern chip body lists internal tool names with code-style commas: 'tool-use loops (search_products, update_draft, select_product)'. Underscored function names exposed to Apex.
findings.fix: Replace underscores with spaces in tool names. 'tool-use loops (search_products, update_draft, select_product)' → 'tool-use loops (search products · update draft · select product)'. Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 242.

findings.issue: Zone header label inside the SVG reads 'Workflow · 6 stages · Temporal-orchestrated'. Same 'Temporal-orchestrated' phrase as H1, repeated visibly inside the diagram.
findings.fix: Replace with 'Workflow · 6 stages · Durable orchestration'. Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 226.

findings.issue: Step 04 says 'assembled via FFmpeg with xfade transitions' and step 05 leads with 'FFmpeg overlays text, colour-grades, applies film grain'. FFmpeg is a brand name (KEEP per §7) but 'xfade' is FFmpeg's internal filter-name jargon.
findings.fix: Step 04: 'assembled via FFmpeg with xfade transitions' → 'assembled with cross-fade scene transitions'. Step 05: keep FFmpeg name but pair with verb — 'FFmpeg overlays text…' → 'Video editor overlays text, colour-grades, applies film grain' (or keep FFmpeg if the bracketed model parenthetical '(Vertex AI Imagen, 9:16)' is also dropped per separate L finding). Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml lines 219-220.
findings.verdict: TRANSLATE (xfade) · KEEP FFmpeg with paired verb

findings.issue: Step 01 detail ends with 'The Brief becomes the entry point of `ContentProductionWorkflow` (Temporal).' Internal class name + parenthetical product name — engineering aside that adds no value to the reader who isn't building the system.
findings.fix: Replace with: 'The brief kicks off the content production workflow.' Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 216.

findings.issue: Step 03 says 'Vertex AI Imagen or fal.ai (`nano-banana-pro`)'. The `nano-banana-pro` is a model codename inside backticks. Step 05 says '(Vertex AI Imagen, 9:16)' — model name + aspect-ratio engineering aside.
findings.fix: Step 03: drop the codename → 'Vertex AI Imagen or fal.ai'. Step 05: drop the parenthetical → 'stitches an AI-generated CTA frame — single product card, multi-product grid, or brand outro'.

findings.issue: Last gate reads 'LLM resilience · per-provider circuit breakers + fallback chain (Claude → OpenAI → Gemini → Vertex)'. 'LLM' + 'circuit breakers' is engineering shorthand. The user-value is 'AI fallback if a provider fails'.
findings.fix: Replace with: 'AI provider resilience · automatic fallback chain (Claude → OpenAI → Gemini → Vertex) when any provider fails'. Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 277 (evals.gates[3]).

findings.issue: Step 02 ends with 'Routes through Claude (primary) with OpenAI / Gemini fallback chain.' Brand names KEEP per §7, but 'fallback chain' is engineering shorthand.
findings.fix: Replace 'fallback chain' with 'as backup'. Final: 'Routes through Claude (primary) with OpenAI / Gemini as backup.' Edit data/agents/generative-media.yaml line 217.

section_04_patterns: M6 (Tool Calling chip · underscored fn names)

verdicts_summary.KEEP: All brand names paired with reader-recognisable verbs — Claude · OpenAI · Gemini · Vertex AI Imagen · fal.ai · Veo · Kling · Runway · Pixelbin · FFmpeg (when paired with 'video editor') · Langfuse (when paired with 'traces')
verdicts_summary.TRANSLATE: 11 occurrences · Temporal → durable orchestration · SDK → widget · GCS → cloud storage · Redis-cached → cached · JWT → access token · LongFormVideoWorkflow → long-form videos · semaphore-bounded → max N concurrent · LineageCollector / RCAAnalyzer → grading reports / root-cause analysis · xfade → cross-fade · LLM resilience → AI provider resilience
verdicts_summary.DROP: 3 occurrences · `nano-banana-pro` codename · `(Vertex AI Imagen, 9:16)` model+aspect parenthetical · code-style backticks around field names like `narrative_arc` / `scenes[]`

strengths: All 4 prior-audit findings (H1 30+ VLM checks · M1 AI Photoshoot input chain · M2 'when it lands' · M3 4-10s union) verified resolved on this delta scan — fixes landed cleanly.

Brand names (Claude · OpenAI · Gemini · Vertex AI Imagen · fal.ai · Veo · Kling · Runway · Pixelbin · FFmpeg · Langfuse) are correctly used as proper nouns where they appear; the issue is placement + framing, not the names themselves.
Numbers verified clean across the page: 6 sub-agents · 6 stages · 9 providers · 4-8s Veo · 5-10s Kling · 60s cache · 15-min token · 68% test coverage.
Honesty register stays solid post-prior-fixes: Building pill matches state, AI Photoshoot integration consistently labelled 'planned', no Reliance overclaim.
Workflow SVG matches AI Photoshoot precedent and renders clean on desktop + mobile (hidden below 768px via @media rule).
recommendation: Apply H1+H2+H3 (hero zone · highest-read · 3 fixes) before sharing with Apex. M1-M8 should land as a sweep — they're all mechanical text replacements. L1-L4 are polish; do them while you're already in the YAML for the M-batch. Net change after full sweep: tone_of_voice 78 → 92, overall 87 → 92 (A range).
